title: hymenophyllum subdemissum
intro: Hymenophyllum subdemissum, also known as the creeping fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta, characterized by its delicate and lacy fronds. The creeping fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be utilized to treat wounds and soothe skin irritations. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
